mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on Print and Payment History page when a plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages, additional pages, progress bars, tooltips, and page count displays.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: This batch contains the test execution framework (YamlStepExecutor, test_case_execute) but does not contain API helpers or methods for printer enrollment. Enrollment methods would likely be in a different batch containing HP Instant Ink API utilities or setup helpers.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription verification or API helper methods found in this batch. This batch focuses on test execution framework rather than HP Instant Ink domain-specific functionality.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification methods found in this batch. API helpers for subscription management would be in a different batch.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.

components_found_in_this_batch.method_signature: def execute_click_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None
components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Executes a click action on a UI element based on the step definition. Extracts element_id, label, page, text, expand, parent, and multiple parameters from the step dictionary. Loads element metadata for the specified page. Handles navigation element expansion detection. Finds the control by text or label using get_control. Handles iframe switching if the element is in an iframe. Calls click_control to perform the actual click with support for multiple element clicking.
components_found_in_this_batch.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver, metadata_filepath='/path/to/metadata', page='instantink/Dashboard')
result = executor.execute_click_action({'action': 'click', 'page': 'instantink/Dashboard', 'text': 'Pause Plan', 'multiple': 'False'})
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method provides the generic click action capability needed to click the Pause Plan button or Pause Instant Ink link. However, it requires the element metadata (locators) to be defined in a JSON metadata file, which is not present in this batch. The method itself is reusable but needs the specific locators for the pause plan elements.

components_found_in_this_batch.method_signature: def click_control(self, control, multiple, timeout: int = 10)
components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Attempts to click UI elements using multiple locator strategies with progressive fallback. Validates control metadata dictionary. Builds strategies list prioritizing XPath selectors first, then CSS selectors, then other strategies including id, name, aria_label, data_testid, class_name, and partial_link_text. Iterates through strategies calling do_clicks for each until one succeeds. Returns True if any strategy successfully clicks the element, False if all fail.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This is the core click execution method that would be used to click the Pause Plan button. It provides robust fallback through multiple locator strategies. However, it requires the control metadata dictionary with locators, which would need to be defined in the page object metadata files not present in this batch.

precondition_mapping.notes: Generic click action methods are available in YamlStepExecutor, but the specific locators for Pause Plan button and Pause Instant Ink link are not defined in this batch. The click capability exists but needs element metadata from page object files.

matched_methods.method_signature: def execute_click_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None
matched_methods.what_it_does: Executes a click action on a UI element based on YAML step definition. Loads page metadata, handles navigation expansion, finds control by text or label, switches to iframe if needed, and performs click with support for multiple elements.
matched_methods.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver, metadata_filepath='/path/to/metadata', page='instantink/PrintPaymentHistory')
result = executor.execute_click_action({'action': 'click', 'page': 'instantink/PrintPaymentHistory', 'text': 'Billing Cycle Period', 'multiple': False})
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides generic click action capability for navigating to Print and Payment History page (steps 1, 4) and clicking pause plan button (precondition 3). Requires element metadata to be defined separately.

matched_methods.method_signature: def click_control(self, control, multiple, timeout: int = 10)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Core click execution method with progressive fallback through multiple locator strategies (XPath, CSS, id, name, aria_label, data_testid, class_name, partial_link_text). Validates control metadata and attempts each strategy until one succeeds.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides robust click capability with fallback strategies for clicking navigation links, info icons, and other interactive elements. Used by execute_click_action internally.

matched_methods.method_signature: def execute_verify_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None
matched_methods.what_it_does: Executes AI-powered visual verification using Azure OpenAI vision model. Takes screenshot if specified, constructs reference image path, formats prompt with variable substitution, calls run_ai_task for AI analysis, and logs results.
matched_methods.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver)
result = executor.execute_verify_action({'action': 'verify', 'id': 'verify_billing_card', 'screenshot': True, 'prompt': 'Verify Billing Cycle Period card displays plan pause information with correct page counts', 'reference_image': 'billing_card_ref.png'})
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method provides AI-powered visual verification capability that could be used to verify all the visual aspects of the test case including billing cycle card display, progress bar colors and fill states, tooltip content, page count displays, and overall UI structure. The AI model can analyze screenshots and validate complex visual requirements that would be difficult with traditional element-based assertions.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Performs AI-powered visual verification using Azure OpenAI vision model. Loads reference and test images, encodes to base64, constructs messages for API call, calls Azure OpenAI chat completions API with vision model, handles streaming or non-streaming responses, parses JSON result, and returns validation data.
matched_methods.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver)
result = executor.run_ai_task(reference='/path/to/reference.png', test='/path/to/test.png', prompt='Verify the complimentary pages progress bar is filled with black color and displays 6 of 10 used')
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Core AI verification engine that can validate complex visual requirements including progress bar colors (black for complimentary, yellow for additional), fill percentages, tooltip content, text displays, and overall UI layout. This is particularly valuable for this test case which has extensive visual verification requirements.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of the current browser page and saves it to the specified file path. Returns the filename.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Used to capture screenshots for AI-powered visual verification. Essential for verifying progress bar states, colors, tooltips, and page count displays throughout the test.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Sets multiple variables in the executor's variables dictionary for use in test steps. Logs each key-value pair and updates the variables dictionary. Returns True.
matched_methods.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver)
executor.set_variables(PRINT_PAYMENT_HISTORY_URL='https://app.hp.com/instant-ink/print-payment-history', PAGES_TO_PRINT_FIRST=6, PAGES_TO_PRINT_SECOND=9, COMPLIMENTARY_LIMIT=10)
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides variable management for storing test data like URLs, page counts, and configuration values that can be referenced throughout the test using ${variable_name} syntax.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Replaces variable placeholders in text with actual values from the variables dictionary. Uses regex to find ${variable_name} patterns and replaces them. Supports dot notation for nested dictionary access (e.g., ${temp_id.status}).
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Enables dynamic test data by allowing YAML steps to reference variables. Used for URLs, page counts, expected values, and other test data that may vary between test runs.

matched_methods.method_signature: def execute_step(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None
matched_methods.what_it_does: Central dispatcher that executes a single test step by routing to the appropriate action handler based on action type. Validates step dictionary, extracts action and page, updates current page, dispatches to action-specific methods (click, set, verify, navigate, etc.), handles sleep delays between steps, and returns result.
matched_methods.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver, metadata_filepath='/path/to/metadata')
result = executor.execute_step({'action': 'click', 'page': 'instantink/PrintPaymentHistory', 'text': 'Billing Cycle Period', 'sleep': 2})
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Core step execution dispatcher that would execute each test step in the test case. This is the main execution engine that routes each action to its appropriate handler.

matched_methods.method_signature: def execute_flow(self, yaml_data: Dict[str, Any], flow_result = None) -> None
matched_methods.what_it_does: Executes a complete test flow defined in YAML data. Validates YAML structure, iterates through all steps, executes each step with timing and error handling, collects results, generates CSV report with step-level timing and status, and prints execution summary.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Orchestrates the complete test execution lifecycle. This method would execute the entire test case flow from start to finish, handling all 15 steps with timing, error handling, and result reporting.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Loads UI element metadata from a JSON file into the control_map dictionary. Splits page identifier to extract module and file name, looks up folder name in metadata dictionary, constructs JSON file path, reads and parses JSON file, and stores in control_map.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Loads element metadata for the Print and Payment History page. Required before interacting with elements on that page. However, the actual JSON metadata files with locators are not in this batch.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Searches for a UI control in the loaded metadata by matching its text or label_text. Iterates through control_map['interactive_elements'] and returns the control dictionary if text or label_text matches the provided name.
matched_methods.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver, metadata_filepath='/path/to/metadata')
executor.load_json_metadata('instantink/PrintPaymentHistory')
control = executor.get_control('Billing Cycle Period')
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Retrieves element metadata by visible text or label. Used to find controls like billing cycle card, progress bars, info icons, and tooltips. Requires metadata to be loaded first.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Sets a value in a UI control (input field or dropdown) using multiple locator strategies with fallback. Handles both text inputs (clear + send_keys) and select dropdowns (select by visible text or value). Tries CSS selectors, XPath selectors, and other strategies until one succeeds.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: While this test case doesn't have explicit input fields, this method could be useful if there were fields to enter page counts or other values. Not directly applicable to current test steps but part of the framework.

matched_methods.method_signature: def do_clicks(self, strategy, multiple, timeout, delay=0.10)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Performs the actual clicking of elements located by a specific strategy. Waits for elements to be present, iterates through found elements, checks display and enabled state, attempts click with exception handling, supports clicking multiple elements with delay, and returns success status.
matched_methods.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver)
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
success = executor.do_clicks((By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.info-icon'), multiple=False, timeout=10)
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Core click execution method used internally by click_control. Handles the actual WebDriver click operations with wait and retry logic. Used for clicking info icons and other interactive elements.

matched_methods.method_signature: def load_yaml_file(self, file_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]
matched_methods.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML file from the filesystem. Opens file with UTF-8 encoding, uses yaml.safe_load to parse content, returns parsed dictionary or empty dict if file is empty. Raises FileNotFoundError or ValueError on errors.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Used to load the YAML test case definition file. The test case C44873414 would need to be written as a YAML file and loaded using this method.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Pytest fixture with module scope that creates and manages a browser instance for the entire test module. Creates MultiBrowserManager, calls create_normal_browser with profile 'instantink', yields driver to test, and calls driver.quit() in cleanup.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides the browser driver instance needed for test execution. This fixture would be used to create the browser for running the billing cycle test case.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Main test function that executes a YAML-defined test case flow. Loads config, retrieves printer info, creates YamlStepExecutor, creates trace folder, sets global variables, loads YAML flow, and calls executor.execute_flow to run the test with result capture.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Main pytest test entry point that would execute the billing cycle test case. This function orchestrates the complete test execution from setup through execution to result capture.

matched_methods.method_signature: def create_normal_browser(self, profile_name: str, user_data_dir: str = None)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Creates a new Chrome browser instance in normal mode with extensive automation detection suppression. Adds arguments to hide automation indicators, configures preferences to disable credential service and notifications, creates Chrome WebDriver, stores driver in browsers registry with metadata, and returns driver instance.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Creates the browser instance used for test execution. The driver fixture in test_case_execute.py uses this method to create the browser.

matched_methods.method_signature: def switch_to_window_by_url(self, url_part: str, exact_match: bool = False)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Searches for and switches to the first window that contains or matches the specified URL across all browsers. Iterates through all browsers and windows, checks URL match (exact or partial), switches to matching window, sets current_browser, and returns dictionary with browser_name, window_handle, url, and driver.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Could be used to switch to the Print and Payment History page window if it opens in a new window or tab. Useful for multi-window test scenarios.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Core test execution engine that processes YAML test definitions and executes test steps. Manages UI element metadata, handles variable substitution, executes various action types, integrates with AI vision models for visual verification, and tracks test execution results. Provides comprehensive framework for YAML-based test automation.
matched_helpers.usage_example:
executor = YamlStepExecutor(manager, driver, metadata_filepath='/path/to/metadata', page='instantink/PrintPaymentHistory')
executor.set_variables(URL='https://app.hp.com', PAGES_TO_PRINT=6)
yaml_data = executor.load_yaml_file('test_cases/billing_cycle_test.yaml')
executor.execute_flow(yaml_data, flow_result='/path/to/results.csv')
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This is the main test execution engine that would be used to run the billing cycle test case. It provides all the core capabilities needed: click actions, visual verification with AI, variable management, step execution, and flow orchestration. The test case would be written as a YAML file and executed using this class.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Manages multiple browser instances with different profiles and modes. Provides methods to create browsers, switch between them, bring windows to foreground, find windows by URL, wait for new windows, load URLs in specific browsers, and manage browser lifecycle. Supports OS-specific window management operations.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: Provides browser management capabilities including browser creation, window switching, and URL loading. Used by the test execution framework to manage the browser instance during test execution.

test_case_mappings.overall_assessment: This batch contains the core YAML-based test execution framework (YamlStepExecutor, MultiBrowserManager, test_case_execute) which provides the infrastructure to run the test case, but does NOT contain the domain-specific components needed for HP Instant Ink functionality. The framework provides: (1) Generic action methods (click, set, verify, navigate) that can be used once element metadata is provided, (2) AI-powered visual verification capability which is EXCELLENT for this test case's extensive visual validation requirements (progress bar colors, tooltips, text displays), (3) Variable management for test data, (4) Test flow orchestration and result reporting. However, the batch is MISSING: (1) Page objects for Print and Payment History page and Dashboard page, (2) Locators for billing cycle card, progress bars, tooltips, info icons, and page count elements, (3) API helpers for printer enrollment, subscription management, time shifting, billing triggers, and page printing simulation, (4) Constants for URLs, page limits, and expected values. The test case would need to be written as a YAML file similar to TC31165176_M_overview_status_card.yaml and would leverage the AI verification capability extensively. The AI-powered verification is particularly well-suited for this test case because it can validate complex visual requirements like progress bar colors, fill states, tooltip content, and text displays that would be difficult with traditional element-based assertions. The missing components (page objects, locators, API helpers) would be found in other batches containing HP Instant Ink domain-specific code.
